subject: URGENT: Login failures affecting our entire team
body:
Hi TechFlow Support,

Since this morning, our entire team of 45 users is unable to log in to the platform from mobile devices. We're getting 'Authentication token validation failed' errors.

This is critical for us as we rely on the mobile app for field operations. Please treat this as P0.

We need an immediate fix or a workaround.

Regards,
John Davis
CTO, Acme Corp

subject: SSO Integration Requirements - Contract Signed
body:
Dear Sunita,

Following our contract signing, we need to discuss the SSO integration timeline. Our requirements:

1. SAML 2.0 support
2. Azure AD integration
3. Role mapping from our directory
4. MFA enforcement
5. Session management

We expect this to be completed within 2 weeks as per our agreement. Can we schedule a kickoff meeting this week?

Best regards,
Sarah Chen
VP Engineering, GlobalTech

subject: RE: Production API Credentials Request
body:
Hi Sanjay,

Thank you for your request. Your production API credentials are currently under review by our compliance team. Expected turnaround is 2-3 business days.

In the meantime, you can continue testing with the sandbox credentials provided earlier.

Please note: Production credentials require PCI DSS compliance documentation from your side. Can you share your latest compliance certificate?

Regards,
Razorpay Partner Team

subject: Load Testing Results - Payment Webhooks
body:
Hi Rahul,

Load testing results for the payment webhook endpoints:

- 500 concurrent: All passing (avg 120ms)
- 750 concurrent: 3% failures (avg 450ms)
- 1000 concurrent: 12% failures (avg 1.2s, some timeouts)

Bottleneck appears to be the database writes during webhook processing. We need to either:
1. Add connection pooling for webhook handlers
2. Queue webhook processing asynchronously
3. Increase database connection limits

Recommendation: Option 2 (async queue) would give us the best scalability.

Please discuss with the team and let me know the approach.

Regards,
Kavita

subject: CRITICAL: XSS Vulnerability - User Profile Page
body:
Hi Priya,

Our automated security scan has detected a critical XSS vulnerability:

- Severity: High (CVSS 7.5)
- Location: /profile/{id} endpoint
- Parameter: bio field
- Impact: Arbitrary JavaScript execution in victim's browser
- Discovery: Automated scan on 2026-06-16

This needs to be patched within 24 hours per our security policy.

Required Actions:
1. Sanitize user input before rendering
2. Implement Content Security Policy
3. Add output encoding
4. Validate input on save

Please confirm the fix timeline.

Security Team

subject: Feature Request: PDF Export for Analytics
body:
Hi TechFlow,

We love the analytics dashboard but our management team needs the ability to export reports as PDF documents for board meetings.

Requirements:
- Export individual charts as images
- Generate full dashboard PDF report
- Include date range and filters in the export
- Brand customization (our logo, colors)
- Scheduled reports via email

This would be very valuable for our team. Please consider for your roadmap.

Best,
John Davis
CTO, Acme Corp

2. express: CVE-2024-29041 (High - Open redirect)
3. follow-redirects: CVE-2024-28849 (High - Authorization header leak)

Review and merge the automated PRs to resolve these vulnerabilities.

subject: Product Catalog Caching - Requirements Update
body:
Hi Vikram,

Following our discussion, here are the updated requirements for the product catalog caching layer:

1. Cache TTL: 5 minutes for listings, 15 minutes for categories
2. Cache invalidation: Real-time on product update via events
3. Cache warming: Pre-populate top 1000 products on deployment
4. Fallback: Serve stale cache if database is unavailable (circuit breaker)
5. Metrics: Cache hit/miss ratio, latency improvement, memory usage

Please create a technical design document before implementation.

Thanks,
Tarun

subject: WebSocket Architecture Proposal - Draft
body:
Hi Anil,

Here's my initial draft for the WebSocket architecture:

Option A: Socket.IO with Redis adapter
- Pros: Easy to implement, good ecosystem
- Cons: Not great for 10k+ connections
- Cost: ~$200/month additional infrastructure

Option B: Native WebSocket with Go server
- Pros: High performance, low resource usage
- Cons: More development time
- Cost: ~$100/month

Option C: Managed service (Pusher/Ably)
- Pros: Zero maintenance, instant scaling
- Cons: Vendor lock-in, costs scale with usage
- Cost: ~$500-2000/month depending on usage

Recommendation: Option B for production, Option A for POC.

I'll have the full document ready by Thursday.

Deepak

subject: Sprint Retrospective Notes - Sprint 24
body:
Hi Anil,

Key takeaways from today's retro:

What went well:
- User management API delivered on time
- Good collaboration between backend and frontend on analytics

What didn't go well:
- Too many production incidents disrupted sprint work
- Payment integration blocked for 5 days waiting for vendor
- Several tickets had poor descriptions (PROJ-1003, PROJ-1006, PROJ-1011)

Action Items:
1. @anil.pradhan: Establish vendor SLA for credential delivery
2. @rahul.sharma: Create ticket template with mandatory fields
3. @ravi.shankar: Set up automated certificate renewal
4. @sneha.nair: Document DB connection pool monitoring runbook

Please ensure these are tracked.

Rahul

subject: Email Notification Service - Kafka Topic Setup
body:
Hi Sneha,

Can you help me set up the following Kafka topics for the email notification service?

1. email-send (partitions: 6, retention: 7 days)
2. email-bounce (partitions: 3, retention: 30 days)
3. email-delivered (partitions: 3, retention: 7 days)
4. email-dlq (dead letter queue, partitions: 1, retention: 90 days)

I also need consumer group permissions for the email-notification-service.

Can we get this done by tomorrow? I'm blocked on the service implementation until the topics are ready.

Thanks,
Amit

subject: Test Plan: Password Reset Flow
body:
Hi Kavita,

Here's the test plan for the password reset flow:

Positive Cases:
1. Normal password reset flow
2. Token expiration handling
3. Password strength validation

Negative Cases:
1. Invalid email address
2. Expired token usage
3. Reused token
4. Concurrent reset requests
5. Rate limiting enforcement
6. SQL injection in email field

Edge Cases:
1. User with no email
2. Simultaneous password resets from different devices
3. Token used after password already changed

I'll have the automated tests ready by tomorrow.

Nitin
